2603.17884.title: DebugLM: Learning Traceable Training Data Provenance for LLMs
2603.17884.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are trained through multi-stage pipelines over heterogeneous data sources, yet developers lack a principled way to pinpoint the specific data responsible for an observed behavior. This lack of observability reduces debugging to reactive patching and makes failures prone to recur under distribution shift or subsequent model updates. To address this limitation, we propose DebugLM, a framework that equips LLMs with built-in data provenance, enabling them to explicitly trace the origins of their behaviors to specific training data sources. Specifically, the model learns to associate its responses with unique provenance tags that indicate the responsible dataset, empowering developers to precisely identify where undesirable behaviors are learned. Building on this capability, DebugLM further supports targeted test-time remediation, enabling developers to selectively trigger targeted refusal for specified data sources without retraining or modifying model parameters. Experiments demonstrate that DebugLM provides accurate behavior tracing in multi-stage training pipelines and effective test-time remediation while preserving the general utility of the model.
2603.17884.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）通過多階段管道在異質數據源上進行訓練，但開發者缺乏一種原則性的方式來確定導致觀察到的行為的具體數據。這種缺乏可觀察性使得除錯變成了反應式修補，並使得在分佈變化或後續模型更新下故障容易重現。為了解決這一限制，我們提出了DebugLM，這是一個為LLMs提供內建數據來源的框架，使它們能夠明確追溯其行為的來源到特定的訓練數據源。具體而言，模型學會將其回應與獨特的來源標籤關聯，這些標籤指示負責的數據集，使開發者能夠精確識別不良行為的學習來源。在此能力的基礎上，DebugLM進一步支持針對性的測試時修復，使開發者能夠選擇性地對指定的數據源觸發針對性拒絕，而無需重新訓練或修改模型參數。實驗表明，DebugLM在多階段訓練管道中提供準確的行為追蹤和有效的測試時修復，同時保留模型的一般效用。
2603.17884.author: Wenjie Jacky Mo et.al.
2603.17884.authors: Wenjie Jacky Mo, Qin Liu, Xiaofei Wen, Wenxuan Zhou, Zhe Zhao, Muhao Chen
